--- format: revealjs: incremental: true slide-number: true progress: true code-line-numbers: true code-copy: true scrollable: true theme: - EDS213_slides.scss embed-resources: true --- ## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#cad2c5"} [EDS 213 Lab 2]{.custom-title}
[Data Cleaning]{.custom-subtitle} ## {#overview data-menu-title="Overview"} [Today's Lab]{.slide-title} Real-world datasets are messy. We'll walk through **three example databases** and the cleaning steps each one needs before ingesting into a database. ::: {.cell} ::: ## {#Database1 data-menu-title="Database # 1"} [Database #1]{.slide-title} :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="55%"} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Air Quality
Date Location PM2_5 NO2 O3
2020-01-01 Site A 12.3 20 15
2020/02/01 Site B fifteen 30 20
March 3, 2020 Site C 7.8 25 twenty
NA Site A 9 NA 30
2020-04-01 Site D 10.2 40 35
````` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column width="45%"} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Water Quality
Sample_Date Site_Name pH Turbidity Lead_Concentration
01-01-2020 River X 6.5 3.2 0.05
2020-02-01 Lake Y 7 2.8 0.07
03-03-2020 River X 7.5 NA 0.1
2020-Apr-04 Lake Z 7.2 3.5 0.04
2020-05-01 River X 8.1 300.0 0.030 ppm
````` ::: ::: ::: :::: ## {#Database1-issues data-menu-title="Database # 1 Issues"} [Database #1: What's Wrong?]{.slide-title} **Air Quality table:** - `Date` — mixed formats: `2020-01-01`, `2020/02/01`, `March 3, 2020` - `PM2_5` — contains `"fifteen"` instead of a number - `NO2` — contains `"NA"` as a string, not a true missing value **Water Quality table:** - `Sample_Date` — multiple inconsistent date formats - `Lead_Concentration` — mixes plain numbers with `"0.030 ppm"` ## {#Database2 data-menu-title="Database # 2"} [Database #2]{.slide-title} :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="45%"} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Air Quality
Date Time Location PM10 PM2_5 NO2
2023-04-01 08:00 City Park 50 25 µg/m³ 40
2023-04-01 14:00 City Park 55 30 42
2023-04-02 08:00 Industrial Area 150 75 60
2023-04-02 14:00 Industrial Area 155 mg/m³ 80 NA
2023-04-03 08:00 Residential Zone 40 NA 35
````` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column width="55%"} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Weather (wide format)
Location X2023.04.01.Temp.06.00 X2023.04.01.Humidity.06.00 X2023.04.01.Temp.12.00 X2023.04.01.Humidity.12.00 X2023.04.01.Temp.18.00 X2023.04.01.Humidity.18.00 X2023.04.01.Temp.24.00 X2023.04.01.Humidity.24.00
Park 14 80 22 60 20 70 16 85
Beach 16 85 24 55 22 75 18 90
Forest 13 90 21 65 19 80 15 95
Hill 15 75 23 50 21 65 17 80
````` ::: ::: ::: :::: ## {#Database2-issues data-menu-title="Database # 2 Issues"} [Database #2: What's Wrong?]{.slide-title} **Air Quality table:** - `PM10` has one entry with `"155 mg/m³"` — unit embedded in value - `PM2_5` has one entry with `"25 µg/m³"` — same problem **Weather table:** - Data is in **wide format** — each column encodes date, variable, and time - Relational databases need **long/tidy format** - Need `pivot_longer()` to reshape into separate `Date`, `Time`, `Temp`, and `Humidity` columns ## {#Database3 data-menu-title="Database # 3"} [Database #3]{.slide-title} :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="60%"} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Daily Air Quality
Date Location PM2_5 NO2 Ozone
2020-01-01 Location1 46.69874 87.96601 98.12719
2020-01-01 Location2 119.30424 33.44394 89.80869
2020-01-01 Location3 64.30165 35.56292 63.59488
2020-01-02 Location1 133.03752 24.92722 88.71676
2020-01-02 Location2 141.36776 12.69610 34.84138
2020-01-02 Location3 11.60569 18.83068 22.21545
````` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column width="40%"} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Yearly Vegetation Health
Year Location VHI
2020 Location1 63.01918
2021 Location1 83.86073
2022 Location1 80.71608
2020 Location2 92.40360
2021 Location2 42.04080
2022 Location2 51.59250
2020 Location3 75.97901
2021 Location3 77.41948
2022 Location3 88.97631
````` ::: ::: ::: :::: ## {#Database3-issues data-menu-title="Database # 3 Issues"} [Database #3: What's Wrong?]{.slide-title} **Daily Air Quality table:** - Recorded at **daily** resolution **Yearly Vegetation Health table:** - Recorded at **yearly** resolution - These tables cannot be joined directly — they have different temporal resolutions **Fix:** Aggregate daily air quality to yearly averages so both tables share a `Year` + `Location` key. ## {#Database3-cleaning data-menu-title="Database # 3 Cleaning"} [Database #3 Cleaning]{.slide-title} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Yearly Air Quality
Year Location Avg_PM2_5 Avg_NO2 Avg_Ozone
2020 Location1 76.88149 53.82688 69.36883
2020 Location2 79.32856 54.50517 71.14231
2020 Location3 74.58713 56.02009 68.87337
2021 Location1 75.65688 55.90514 68.70571
2021 Location2 78.93855 52.15081 71.25445
2021 Location3 77.60456 55.07368 67.97183
````` ::: ::: ## {#Database3-join data-menu-title="Database # 3 Join"} [Database #3: Joining the Tables]{.slide-title} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Joined: Air Quality + Vegetation Health
Year Location Avg_PM2_5 Avg_NO2 Avg_Ozone VHI
2020 Location1 76.88149 53.82688 69.36883 63.01918
2020 Location2 79.32856 54.50517 71.14231 92.40360
2020 Location3 74.58713 56.02009 68.87337 75.97901
2021 Location1 75.65688 55.90514 68.70571 83.86073
2021 Location2 78.93855 52.15081 71.25445 42.04080
2021 Location3 77.60456 55.07368 67.97183 77.41948
````` ::: ::: Both tables now share the same `Year` + `Location` key — the join works cleanly. ## {#this-weeks-task data-menu-title="This Week's Task"} [This Week's Task]{.slide-title} ::: {style="font-size: 0.8em;"} Apply these principles to **your own dataset**. Before next week's ingestion step, make sure your data meets the following criteria: - All date columns use a **consistent format** - Numeric columns contain **only numbers** (no units or text mixed in) - Missing values are represented consistently as **`NA`** - Column names are **consistent across tables** that will be joined - Data is in **long/tidy format** (each variable in its own column, each observation in its own row) - Tables that will be joined share a **common key** - Any **other cleaning steps** specific to your dataset (e.g., removing duplicates, handling outliers, standardizing category names) :::

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
